page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the 64-bit ARM64 architecture, this instance features the Neoverse-V2 CPU model running at 2.8 GHz. It delivers 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading. The processor includes 64 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong memory subsystem performance, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded performance is also notable, with Geekbench single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scores placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core and integer-heavy benchmarks, such as the PassMark CPU score and stress-ng multi-core performance, fall within the average range. Cryptographic performance presents a bottleneck, as the PassMark CPU encryption test scored in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory write bandwidth is measured at 86.39 GB/sec, while read bandwidth reaches 153.41 GB/sec for large blocks.

The r8gb.2xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores. It is suitable for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and memory-bound data processing tasks. In testing, Redis SET operations reached 17.97 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput achieved 19.9 GB/sec. However, the instance exhibits limitations in machine learning tasks; LLM inference prompt processing for a medium 7B model scored in the bottom 10%, and text generation for a small 135M model ranked in the bottom 25%. The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs further restricts its utility for local high-speed scratch disk operations or heavy parallel computing.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB per core, with no hyperthreading. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but features a 17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and strong single-core processing in the top 25%. However, cryptographic operations and LLM inference tasks show weaker relative performance. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching layers like Redis, where high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores are critical, rather than compute-heavy cryptographic or machine learning workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory delivering 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth performance ranking in the top 10%
